title: Reassessing Ambition
sentences: For most of my career, ambition felt like an unquestionable virtue rather than a choice.
I equated busyness with importance, and exhaustion with proof of commitment.
It took a prolonged period of burnout to make me question that equation.
Stepping back, I noticed how much of my effort was driven by fear rather than purpose.
Colleagues who set firmer boundaries were not, as I had assumed, less accomplished.
Redefining success meant admitting that constant striving was not inherently admirable.
This was, admittedly, an uncomfortable realisation for someone who valued relentlessness.
Since then, I have tried to measure my work by its impact rather than its intensity.
The irony is that my output has scarcely suffered, despite working noticeably less.
What changed most was my relationship with the very idea of enough.
